problem.passage:
4. Read the conversation in <A> and the passage in <B>, and
follow the directions.【2 points】
<A>
(Two teachers, Mr. Lee and Ms. Kim, recently scored students’
speaking assessments. They later discussed the scoring process
and Mr. Lee reflected on his scoring experiences in his journals.)
: As I was reviewing my ratings, I noticed that they Mr. Lee
were staying consistent throughout the scoring
process.
Ms. Kim: Good, it’s actually hard to keep the same
perspective when grading multiple students. But
you mean you found actual similarities in your
scores for the same students over time?
: Yes, exactly. I think I might have benefitted from Mr. Lee
reviewing my previous scores before re-evaluating
anyone’s performance to see if I’m staying consistent.
Kim: That makes sense. You know, I’ve noticed that we Ms.
have some scoring differences between us on
certain criteria.
Mr. Lee : Right. I normally give a score of 10 if students have
natural flow even though they may demonstrate
some errors in grammar or vocabulary. How about
you?
Kim: Oh, I’ve constantly made efforts to adhere to our Ms.
scoring criteria, and I give a perfect score only
when they speak without any errors or hesitation.
: All right. Now I can see why we have different Mr. Lee
think―these different scoring results and it makes me
results could send mixed messages to students.
Ms. Kim: I agree. Let’s review our criteria and stick to
following our rubric.
: Sure. That would be fairer for the students. Mr. Lee
<B>
Mr. Lee’s Reflective Journal
After today’s grading session, I reviewed my scores and
luckily noticed consistency in my ratings for the same students
across different sessions. However, after talking with Ms.
Kim, I realized that we provided different scores for the same
students. I’m concerned this could lead to some confusion if
they receive different scores based on which teacher assesses
them. I think it would be helpful if Ms. Kim and I could go
over the rubric together to ensure a more unified scoring
approach.
Fill in the blanks with the TWO most appropriate words.
Based on <A> and <B>, Mr. Lee is concerned about the lack
of in the scoring process. His
concern is not about the consistency of rating by a single rater
but about the consistency of rating by different raters.
3
(8면 중 면)
